title: SSC CGL 2026 Sectional Timing Tier I 10 10

questions.question: Identify the roll number that does not follow the given rule. Last three digits = First three digits + 150
questions.explanation: Explanation: For A: 120 + 150 = 270 For B: 180 + 150 = 330 For D: 145 + 150 = 295 But for C: 200 + 150 = 350, not 360 [Does not follow]

questions.question: A train passes a platform of length 500 meters in 40 seconds and a bridge of length 800 meters in 55 seconds. What is the length of the train?
questions.explanation: Length of platform = 500 m Length of bridge = 800 m Train covers extra 300 m in 55 - 40 = 15 sec Speed of train = 300/15 = 20 m/sec Now, Length of train + 500 = 20 x 40 Length of train = 800 - 500 = 300 m

questions.question: Eight persons- A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H are sitting around a square table. Four persons are sitting at the corner of the table facing outside the centre and four of them are sitting at the edges of the table facing inside the centre. B sits third to the left of G. Two persons sit between B and H. G does not faces inside the centre. D is an immediate neighbour of H and E. A sits second to the right of F. Who among the following sits third to the right of C?
questions.explanation: Eight persons- A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H are sitting around a square table. Four persons are sitting at the corner of the table facing outside the centre and four of them are sitting at the edges of the table facing inside the centre. B sits third to the left of G. Two persons sit between B and H. G does not faces inside the centre. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30405/image022.png) D is an immediate neighbour of H and E. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30405/image023.png) A sits second to the right of F. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30405/image024.png) Now, only C is remaining. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30405/image025.png) F sits third to the right of C.

questions.question: Martha got married to Jason's wife's brother's wife's mother-in-law's only daughter's son. How is Jason related to Martha?
questions.explanation: Martha got married to Jason's wife's brother's wife's mother-in-law's only daughter's son. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30405/image029.png) Jason is the father-in-law of Martha.

questions.question: Select the letter-cluster from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series. CY, FU, IQ, LM, OI, ?
questions.explanation: Here, the logic is: For the 1st letter of each term: C+3= F; F+3= I; I+3= L; L+3= O, O+3= R. For the 2nd letter of each term: Y-4= U; U-4= Q; Q-4= M; M-4= I; I-4= E.

questions.question: Two letters in the given series are put in bracket. Which of the bracketed letters are correct? L, I, (D), S, F, Q, (X), C, F
options: First bracketed letter is incorrect but the second is correct
Both the bracketed letters are correct
First bracketed letter is correct but the second is incorrect
Both the bracketed letters are incorrect
questions.explanation: Consecutive prime numbers (positions) are added: L + 23 = I I + 19 = B B + 17 = S S + 13 = F F + 11 = Q Q + 7 = X X + 5 = C C + 3 = F First bracketed letter is incorrect but the second is correct.

questions.question: Which letter cluster will replace the question mark (?) in the following series? BALL, YFNR, ?, SPRD, PUTJ
questions.explanation: B (AZ) Y (XW) V (UT) S (RQ) P A (BCDE) F (GHIJ) K (LMNO) P (QRST) U L (M) N (O) P (Q) R (S) T L (MNOPQ) R (STUVW) X (YZABC) D (EFGHI) J

questions.question: Which letter cluster will replace the question mark (?) to complete the given series? GIVE, JMTH, ?, OUPN, QYNQ
questions.explanation: G(HI)J(K)L(MN)O(P)Q I(JKL)M(NOP)Q(RST)U(VWX)Y V(U)T(S)R(Q)P(O)N E(FG)H(IJ)K(LM)N(OP)Q So, 'LQRK' will replace the question mark (?) to complete the given series.

questions.question: In the question below, an assertion (A) is given followed by a reason (R) which may or may not explain the assertion. From the given options, choose the right answer. A: The new employees did not report at 10 am during their first week of joining the organization. R: The induction team forgot to mention the reporting time in the induction e-mails.
options: A is true while R is false
A is false while R is true
Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation for A
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation for A
questions.explanation: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation for A. Since the assertion is a random statement, we assume it to be true. Now, the assertion states that all the new employees missed the reporting time. Hence, the given reason is the correct explanation for the new employees being unaware of the reporting time. Hence, the answer is option c).

questions.question: Select the correct combination of mathematical signs to replace 'm' in the below equation and there by balance it. 18m9m14m7m8m3m6
questions.explanation: From (1): 18 + 9 - 14 ÷ 7 x 8 = 3 + 6 27 - 2 x 8 = 9 27 - 16 = 9 11 = 9 LHS ≠ RHS So, this option is not correct. From (2): 18 ÷ 9 + 14 ÷ 7 - 8 = 3 + 6 2 + 2 - 8 = 9 4 - 8 = 9 -4 = 9 LHS ≠ RHS So, this option is not correct. From (3): 18 ÷ 9 + 14 ÷ 7 x 8 = 3 x 6 2 + 2 x 8 = 18 2 + 16 = 18 18 = 18 LHS = RHS So, this option is correct. From (4): 18 + 9 - 14 + 7 - 8 = 3 + 6 34 - 22 = 9 12 = 9 LHS ≠ RHS So, this option is not correct.

questions.question: Which of the following numbers will replace the question mark (?) in the given series? 988 924 ? 839 814 798
questions.explanation: Pattern of the series - 988 - (8)2 = 924 924 - (7)2 = 875 875 - (6)2 = 839 839 - (5)2 = 814 814 - (4)2 = 798 875 will replace the question mark.

questions.question: Read the following statement and answer the following question. 'A % Z' means 'A is the mother-in-law of Z' 'A $ Z' means 'A is the sister of Z' 'A @ Z' means 'A is the father of Z' 'A ! Z' means 'A is the son of Z' In the equation, H @ G ! S % R $ W, how is G related to W?
questions.explanation: It is given that, 'A % Z' means 'A is the mother-in-law of Z' 'A $ Z' means 'A is the sister of Z' 'A @ Z' means 'A is the father of Z' 'A ! Z' means 'A is the son of Z' ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25113/image011.jpg) After decoding, we get: H is the father of G. G is the son of S. S is the mother-in-law of R. R is the sister of W. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25113/image012.jpg) Thus, G is either brother-in-law of W or G is the brother-in-law of W's sister.

questions.question: A situation is given, followed by two conclusions I and II. Read the situation and both conclusions carefully and select the correct answer. Situation: The Boutique X designer creates unique styles and a wide range of clothing. These designer items are made from high-quality, natural materials that keep their shape, due to which the prices of each piece are high. Every person who visits the store likes the collection, but only few of them purchase the clothes. Conclusions: I. Most people visiting the boutique cannot afford these highly-priced clothes. II. Most of the items displayed in Boutique X are not for sale.
options: Both I and II can be concluded
Neither I nor II can be concluded
questions.explanation: The given situation states that, The designer at Boutique X designs a variety of styles and designs. These designer pieces are expensive because they are made of premium, naturally occurring materials that maintain their shape. While most customers who visit the store are impressed by the collection, very few buy anything from it. I. Most people visiting the boutique cannot afford these highly-priced clothes: Some people who can afford it will purchase it because of the high prices. Thus, there are many who cannot afford to purchase these clothes. So, (I) can be concluded. II. Most of the items displayed in Boutique X are not for sale: Designers are creating styles and varieties for sale. So, (II) cannot be concluded.

questions.question: Find the missing term in the following series. T2P, Y7U, D12Z, ____, N22J
If 9 A 2 B 3 = 6 and 12 A 6 B 4 = 18, then the value of 8 A 4 B 2 is:
questions.explanation: Assume A = X and B = ÷ 9 X 2 ÷ 3 = 18 ÷ 3 = 6 12 X 6 ÷ 4 = 72 ÷ 4 = 18 Therefore 8 A 4 B 2 = 8 X 4 ÷ 2 = 32 ÷ 2 = 16

questions.question: In a certain code language, 'this is leap year' is written as 'po mz ad yu'; 'this is july' is written as 'po ks mz' and 'is july normal year' is written as 'ks tw po yu'. How is 'leap' written in that language?
questions.explanation: It is given that, 'this is leap year' is written as 'po mz ad yu' -----(I) 'this is july' is written as 'po ks mz' -----(II) 'is july normal year' is written as 'ks tw po yu' -----(III) From I, II and III: is = po From I and II: this = mz From I and III: year = yu From II and III: july = ks From III: normal = tw From I: leap = ad Thus, 'leap' is written as 'ad' in that language.

questions.question: The sentence below has a word in which the letters are jumbled up. Rearrange the letters of that word, written in capital letters, to form the correct word. When a video game villain gets fed up with being a baddie, he decides to fulfil his RADEM of being good.
questions.explanation: Meaning of - ARMED = Weapon DREAM = A series of events or pictures which happen in your mind while you are asleep. DERMA = Relating to the skin MADRE = Mother Thus, the sentence will be - When a video game villain gets fed up with being a baddie, he decides to fulfil his DREAM of being good.

questions.question: Select the number from the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series. 8, 14, 39, 152, 755, ?
questions.explanation: The next number in the sequence is 4524. Hence, 'd' is the correct option. 8x2-2=14 14x3-3=39 39x4-4=152 152x5-5=755 755x6-6=4524

questions.question: In the following question, select the option which shows the odd word.
questions.explanation: All except notepad are reading materials and notepad is a writing material.

questions.question: P is the father of S. T is the mother of U. R is the daughter of Q. S is the brother of R. P is the son of T. How U is related to S?
questions.explanation: P is the father of S. S is the brother of R. P is the son of T. T is the mother of U. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p22194/image019.png) R is the daughter of Q. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p22194/image020.png) U is either the uncle or aunt of S.

questions.question: Three horses are walking in the direction of a moving train. A passenger in the train sees that one of the horses is Black and the 2 are white. Which of the following can never be true?
options: All three can be partially white and partially black.
There are two white horses and one black horse.
There are two partially black and one fully black horse.
There are two black and one partially black horse.
questions.explanation: Option (a): All three can be partially white and partially black: this means there are two horses white and one-horse is black which is possible. Option (b): There are two white horses and one black horse: this is true Option (c): There are two partially black and one fully black horse: Which means there can be two white horses and one black horse which is possible. Option (d): There are two black and one partially black horse: which means there is one white horse which is not possible. This is not true. So, Option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statement: Only a few Blankets are Sheet Some Napkin is Tissue No Sheet is Napkin Conclusions: I: No Sheet is Tissue II: Some Blankets are not Napkin III: Some Tissue is Blanket
options: Both Conclusion I and II follow
Both Conclusion II and III follow
questions.explanation: Venn diagram: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26550/image014.png) I: No Sheet is Tissue ------ it can be a possibility so does not follow, II: Some Blankets are not Napkin ----- True because it is mentioned Only a few Blankets are Sheet and No Sheet is Napkin. III: Some Tissue is Blanket ------ it does not follow So, only conclusion II follows.

questions.question: In the following question, eight words are given and seven of them are alike in some manner. Select the option which shows the odd word. Liver, Pancreas, Lungs, Heart, Kidney, Mouth, Bone, Brain
questions.explanation: All except Mouth are internal organs of the body and Mouth is an external organ of the body.

questions.question: In a certain coded language, 'SUBSTANCE' is coded as 'SBUSNATEC'. How will 'UNPLUGGED' be coded in that language?
questions.explanation: 'SUBSTANCE' is coded as 'SBUSNATEC' ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p23336/image001.png) Similarly, ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p23336/image002.png) 'UNPLUGGED' will be coded as 'LPNUGGUDE' Thus, the answer is option (d).

questions.question: In a coded inequality, if 'M $ N' means 'M is neither equal to nor smaller than N', then which of the following is true?
questions.explanation: "Neither equal to" → M ≠ N "Nor smaller than" → M ≥ N Only Possibility → M > N

questions.question: What is unique about the construction of the Gommateshwara statue?
questions.explanation: The Gommateshwara statue at Shravanabelagola (Karnataka) is a monolith, meaning it is carved out of a single, massive block of stone. It is not built piece by piece. Instead, the entire 57-foot statue of Bahubali was sculpted from one solid granite rock. This makes it one of the largest free-standing monolithic statues in the world. Further Insights The statue was commissioned in 981 CE by Chavundaraya under the Western Ganga dynasty. It represents Bahubali in a meditative standing posture (Kayotsarga). The Mahamastakabhisheka ceremony is held once every 12 years. Monolithic architecture is also seen in rock-cut temples like Ellora. Granite was chosen for durability and long-lasting preservation.

questions.question: With reference to Inland and Coastal Waterways in India, consider the following statements: i) The total length of India's navigable inland waterways exceeds the total length of its coastline. ii) National Waterway-1 (NW-1) facilitates the movement of heavy cargo like coal and fertilizers specifically along the Brahmaputra River. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: India has approximately 14,500 km of navigable inland waterways (rivers, canals, backwaters, creeks). India's total coastline length (including island territories) is about 7,516 km. National Waterway-1 (NW-1) runs along the Ganga - Bhagirathi - Hooghly river system from Prayagraj to Haldia. The Brahmaputra River is designated as National Waterway-2 (NW-2). Further Insights The Inland Waterways Authority of India (IWAI) was established in 1986. The National Waterways Act, 2016 declared 111 waterways as National Waterways. NW-1 is developed under the Jal Marg Vikas Project. Inland Water Transport (IWT) is more fuel-efficient and environmentally friendly compared to road transport. NW-2 plays a key role in improving connectivity in Northeast India.

questions.question: Who secured the Women's Singles title at the 87th Senior National Badminton Championships held in Vijayawada?
questions.explanation: Surya Charishma Tamiri won the Women's Singles title at the 87th Senior National Badminton Championships held in Vijayawada. She defeated Tanvi Patri in a thrilling final, coming back from a game down to win. Tanvi Patri lost the championship match to Surya Charishma Tamiri. Further Insights The Senior National Badminton Championships are a key domestic event to identify top Indian talent. Vijayawada hosted the 87th edition in December 2025. Surya Charishma's victory marked a significant career milestone for the young shuttler. The championships also feature men's singles, doubles, and mixed doubles events. Winners at this event often go on to represent India internationally.

questions.question: What is the primary ingredient used to make the white pigment in Warli paintings?
questions.explanation: Warli paintings, a traditional tribal art form from Maharashtra, use a distinctive white pigment made from rice paste. The paste is prepared by: Soaking and grinding rice Mixing it with water and natural gum This white pigment is then applied on a mud wall background using a bamboo stick. Further Insights Warli art originates from the Warli tribe of Maharashtra. It uses simple geometric shapes: circles, triangles, and squares. Themes include daily life, farming, hunting, and marriage rituals. The central motif often includes the "Tarpa dance" scene. Traditionally, Warli paintings were done on the walls of huts during special occasions.

questions.question: Examine these two statements carefully and select the correct answer using the codes given below. Assertion (A): In massive medieval Indian structures like the Konark Sun Temple, heavy iron clamps and beams were used to hold giant stone blocks in place Reason (R): Archaeological restoration has revealed that the stability of these massive weights relied on iron dowels and precise stone-interlocking techniques rather than the use of traditional mortar or "glue."
options: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is NOT the correct explanation of (A).
questions.explanation: Assertion (A) is true The Konark Sun Temple (13th century CE, Odisha) and other medieval Indian temples used iron clamps, dowels, and beams to join massive stone blocks. Instead of depending on mortar (like modern cement), the builders used: Iron cramps (U-shaped clamps) Iron dowels Precision-cut interlocking stones These methods improved structural stability, helping the monument withstand environmental stress, Reason (R) is also true. Archaeological studies and restoration work have confirmed that: Stones were cut with extreme precision. Iron dowels were inserted into carved sockets. Blocks were interlocked mechanically. Mortar was either minimally used or not relied upon structurally. (R) correctly explains (A). The use of iron dowels and interlocking stones explains how the structure achieved resilience and stability without mortar. The mechanical locking system distributed weight effectively and added strength - which is why the structure remained stable. Further Insights Konark Sun Temple was built by King Narasimhadeva I of the Eastern Ganga dynasty. Iron beams used in Konark were highly corrosion-resistant due to ancient metallurgical skill. Similar iron clamp techniques were used in Brihadeeswara Temple (Tamil Nadu). Dry masonry (stone without mortar) was common in many Indian temples. Interlocking architecture improves load distribution and reduces collapse risk.

questions.question: Which specific technology allows RISAT series satellites to bypass the limitations of cloud cover and darkness to provide continuous Earth observation?
questions.explanation: The RISAT (Radar Imaging Satellite) series uses Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) technology. SAR is an active remote sensing system, meaning it sends its own microwave signals toward the Earth's surface and measures the reflected signals. Microwave radiation has longer wavelengths compared to visible light. These waves can penetrate cloud cover, rain, and even work during night-time, since they do not depend on sunlight. Further Insights RISAT satellites are primarily used for surveillance, agriculture monitoring, and disaster management. Microwave remote sensing is classified as active sensing, unlike optical remote sensing which is passive. SAR can detect surface roughness, soil moisture, and even movement (Interferometric SAR). RISAT-1 was developed by ISRO, while RISAT-2 had Israeli radar collaboration. Radar wavelengths (like X-band, C-band) determine penetration depth and resolution.

questions.question: What is the official theme for World Human Rights Day observed on 10 December 2025?
questions.explanation: The official theme for World Human Rights Day 2025 is "Human Rights, Our Everyday Essentials." This theme highlights that human rights are not abstract ideals but the foundations of everyday life - like the rights to food, safety, freedom of expression, and dignity that we rely on each day Further Insights World Human Rights Day is observed every year on 10 December to mark the adoption of the Universal Declaration of Human Rights in 1948. The theme "Human Rights, Our Everyday Essentials" underscores that rights like dignity, freedom, and safety are part of daily life. The theme aims to reconnect people with human rights that shape everyday experiences. The day is used globally to raise awareness and inspire action toward protecting human rights. Each year, the UN sets a specific theme to highlight a current focus area in human rights advocacy.

questions.question: Arrange the following Viceroys of India in the correct chronological order of their tenure, from the earliest to the latest: i) Lord Curzon ii) Lord Chelmsford iii) Lord Irwin iv) Lord Mayo Select the correct option:
questions.explanation: Chronological Order: Lord Mayo → 1869 - 1872 Lord Curzon → 1899 - 1905 Lord Chelmsford → 1916 - 1921 Lord Irwin → 1926 - 1931 Further Insights Lord Mayo - Established Mayo College; assassinated in Andaman (only Viceroy killed in office). Lord Curzon - Partition of Bengal (1905); Indian Universities Act (1904). Lord Chelmsford - Montagu - Chelmsford Reforms; Government of India Act 1919; Jallianwala Bagh massacre occurred during his tenure. Lord Irwin - Gandhi - Irwin Pact (1931); Civil Disobedience Movement period. Lord Irwin later became Lord Halifax, a key British political figure.

questions.question: The MedaramJathara, known as the second-largest fair in India after the Kumbh Mela, is primarily celebrated by which tribal community?
questions.explanation: The MedaramJathara (also called Sammakka-Saralamma Jatara) is primarily celebrated by the Koya tribe in the state of Telangana. It is a major tribal festival held biennially in Medaram village (Mulugu district). The festival honors tribal deities Sammakka and Saralamma, who are believed to have fought against unjust rulers to protect their people. The Koya tribe plays a central role in conducting rituals, organizing ceremonies, and preserving the oral traditions associated with the festival. Gond The Gond tribe is one of the largest tribal communities in India, mainly found in central India (Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Maharashtra), Bhil The Bhil tribe mainly resides in Rajasthan, Gujarat, Madhya Pradesh, and Maharashtra. Santhal The Santhal tribe is concentrated in Jharkhand, West Bengal, Odisha, and Bihar. Further Insights MedaramJathara is held once every two years (biennial festival). It is recognized as a State Festival of Telangana. The festival does not involve idol worship in the conventional sense; symbolic representations of deities are used. It attracts crores of devotees, making it one of the largest religious gatherings in the world. The Koya tribe belongs to the Dravidian tribal group and primarily inhabits Telangana, Chhattisgarh, and Andhra Pradesh.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: i) Monocots have only one cotyledon (e.g., gram, pea, beans), while dicots have two (e.g., maize, wheat). ii)Monocots have parallel venation, where the veins run straight and side-by-side from the base to the tip. Dicots have reticulate venation. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 It is true that: Monocots have one cotyledon. Dicots have two cotyledons. However, the examples are reversed in the statement. Correct classification: Monocots: maize, wheat Dicots: gram, pea, beans Statement 2 Monocots generally show parallel venation (e.g., wheat, rice, maize). Dicots generally show reticulate (net-like) venation (e.g., pea, mango, hibiscus). Further Insights Monocots usually have fibrous root systems, dicots have tap roots. Monocot flowers are typically in multiples of 3. Dicot flowers are usually in multiples of 4 or 5. Vascular bundles are scattered in monocots but arranged in a ring in dicots. Most cereals (rice, wheat, maize) are monocots.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution can the Union government give grants to States for public purposes, even on subjects in the State List (basis of Centrally Sponsored Schemes)?
questions.explanation: Article 282 allows both the Union and the States to make grants for any public purpose, even if the subject is not within their legislative domain. This Article forms the constitutional basis of Centrally Sponsored Schemes (CSS) such as: PMGSY MGNREGA National Health Mission Even if a scheme relates to a State List subject (like public health or agriculture), the Union can still provide financial assistance under Article 282. Article 263 deals with the Inter-State Council to resolve disputes and promote coordination. Article 280 deals with the Finance Commission, which recommends distribution of taxes between Centre and States. Article 312 deals with the creation of All India Services like IAS, IPS. Further Insights Centrally Sponsored Schemes are usually cost-shared between Centre and States. Article 282 reflects India's model of cooperative federalism. Finance Commission transfers (Article 280) are different from discretionary grants under Article 282. CSS increased significantly after the Planning Commission era. Article 275 also provides statutory grants to certain States.

questions.question: With reference to Polar Orbits used by imaging satellites, consider the following statements: i) In a Polar Orbit, the satellite remains fixed over a single geographic point on the equator as the Earth rotates. ii) The North - South movement of the satellite is caused directly by the Earth's West - East rotation underneath it. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: A satellite that remains fixed over one point on the equator is in a Geostationary Orbit, not a Polar Orbit. In a Polar Orbit, the satellite travels roughly from North Pole to South Pole while the Earth rotates beneath it. This allows the satellite to cover the entire Earth over time. The North - South motion of a polar satellite is due to its orbital path being nearly perpendicular to the equator, not because of Earth's rotation. The Earth's West - East rotation only helps different areas come under the satellite's path over time. Further Insights Polar orbits usually have an inclination close to 90 degrees. Imaging and remote sensing satellites (like IRS, RISAT) use Sun-synchronous polar orbits. Geostationary satellites orbit at about 36,000 km above the equator. Polar satellites provide global coverage, while geostationary satellites provide continuous regional coverage. Earth rotates from West to East, which allows polar satellites to scan new areas in each orbit.

questions.question: Who is the author of the influential book "The Indian War of Independence 1857", published in 1909?
questions.explanation: "The Indian War of Independence 1857" was written by Vinayak Damodar Savarkar (V.D. Savarkar) and published in 1909. The book was significant because: It described the Revolt of 1857 as a planned national war of independence, not merely a "Sepoy Mutiny." It promoted revolutionary nationalism. The British government banned the book due to its anti-colonial tone. It played an important role in shaping nationalist interpretation of the 1857 revolt. Bal Gangadhar Tilak Tilak wrote works like "Gita Rahasya" and was a strong nationalist leader Further Insights The British called it the Sepoy Mutiny, while Indian nationalists later termed it the First War of Independence. The revolt began in Meerut on 10 May 1857. After the revolt, the Government of India Act 1858 ended Company rule and began Crown rule. The revolt saw participation of leaders like Rani Lakshmibai, Bahadur Shah Zafar, Tantia Tope. Savarkar wrote the book while in London; it was secretly circulated in India.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as the youngest National Working President in the history of the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP)?
questions.explanation: Nitin Nabin has been appointed as the National Working President of the BJP and is the youngest person to hold this position in the party's history. His appointment represents a generational shift in the leadership of the party, reflecting a push towards involving younger leaders in top organisational roles. Further Insights The National Working President is a key organisational position responsible for party management and coordination. Appointment of younger leaders aims to improve youth outreach and organisational dynamism. Senior roles like National President and Working President are crucial in planning national election strategies.

questions.question: Examine these two statements carefully and select the correct answer using the codes given below. Assertion (A): The mushroom we see above ground is only the temporary "fruiting body" of a much larger hidden organism. Reason (R): Edible mushrooms lack the toxic alkaloids and heavy metals that many other fungi produce as a defense mechanism.
options: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is NOT the correct explanation of (A).
questions.explanation: Assertion (A) - True The visible mushroom is just the fruiting body (reproductive structure) of a fungus. The main organism actually exists underground (or inside wood/soil) as a network of thread-like structures called mycelium. The mycelium can spread over a large area, while the mushroom we see is temporary and appears mainly for reproduction (spore dispersal). Reason (R) - True Some edible mushrooms are safe because they do not produce harmful toxins like certain poisonous fungi do (e.g., Amanita species). However, fungi can sometimes accumulate heavy metals from soil, and toxicity depends on species and environment. So broadly speaking, edible mushrooms are considered safe because they lack dangerous toxins present in poisonous species. Further Insights (Fungi & Biology) The largest living organism on Earth is a fungus (Armillaria), spread over several kilometers. Mycelium plays a key role in nutrient recycling in ecosystems. Some fungi form symbiotic relationships with plant roots (mycorrhiza). Poisonous mushrooms like Amanita phalloides contain deadly toxins (amatoxins). Fungi reproduce through spores, not seeds.

questions.question: What is the total length of the rectangular Kabaddi court specifically for the men's category?
questions.explanation: The men's Kabaddi court measures: 13 meters (length) X 10 meters (width) This rectangular court is divided into two equal halves of 6.5 meters each. It also includes: Baulk line Bonus line Midline Lobby area These dimensions are used in professional and international competitions, including the Pro Kabaddi League. Further Insights Women's Kabaddi court measures 12 X 8 meters. A Kabaddi team consists of 7 players on the court and 5 substitutes. A raid must be completed within 30 seconds in professional matches. Kabaddi was included in the Asian Games in 1990, and India has dominated historically.

questions.question: With reference to the natural compound 'Sinapic acid,' recently in the news for its wound-healing properties, consider the following statements: i) It is a phenolic acid derived from cinnamic acid that possesses diverse medicinal properties, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. ii) It aids in the treatment of diabetic foot ulcers by activating the SIRT1 pathway, which promotes tissue repair and the formation of new blood vessels. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 - Correct Sinapic acid is a naturally occurring phenolic compound derived from cinnamic acid. It is found in plants like mustard seeds, cereals, fruits, and vegetables. It is known for: Antioxidant properties (reduces oxidative stress) Anti-inflammatory effects Neuroprotective and antimicrobial potential Statement 2 - Correct Recent research suggests that Sinapic acid may help in wound healing, especially in diabetic foot ulcers. It works by activating the SIRT1 pathway, which: Reduces inflammation Promotes cell survival Encourages angiogenesis (formation of new blood vessels) Enhances tissue repair This is significant because diabetic wounds heal slowly due to poor blood circulation and oxidative stress. Further Insights Sinapic acid belongs to the class of hydroxycinnamic acids. The SIRT1 protein is linked to aging, metabolism, and cellular repair mechanisms. Oxidative stress plays a major role in delayed wound healing in diabetics. Natural phenolic compounds are widely studied for therapeutic applications. Angiogenesis is essential for supplying oxygen and nutrients to healing tissues.

questions.question: Which of the following Tamil epics is popularly known as "The Tale of the Anklet"?
questions.explanation: Silappadikaram literally means "The Story (Adikaram) of the Anklet (Silambu)". It is one of the Five Great Tamil Epics and was composed by Ilango Adigal. The story revolves around Kannagi, whose husband Kovalan is wrongly executed by the Pandya king. Kannagi proves his innocence by showing her anklet (silambu), which becomes the central symbol of justice and truth. The epic highlights: Moral justice Role of women Sangam-era trade and urban life Political and social systems of ancient Tamilakam Manimekalai Manimekalai is another Tamil epic and is considered a sequel to Silappadikaram. It focuses on Buddhist philosophy and the daughter of Kovalan and Madhavi. Valayapathi Valayapathi is one of the Five Great Tamil Epics, but much of it is lost. It is believed to have had Jain influence CivakaCintamani CivakaCintamani is a Jain epic that narrates the adventures of Jivaka (Civakan) and emphasizes ascetic ideals. Further Insights The Five Great Tamil Epics are: Silappadikaram, Manimekalai, CivakaCintamani, Valayapathi, and Kundalakesi. Silappadikaram provides valuable details about Chola, Chera, and Pandya kingdoms. Kannagi is revered as a symbol of chastity and justice in Tamil culture. The epic reflects strong Jain influence, especially in its philosophical undertones. It gives insights into ancient Tamil trade networks with Rome and Southeast Asia.

questions.question: Which two scientists are credited with the groundbreaking discoveries that electric currents create magnetic fields and that moving magnets can induce electric currents?
options: Hans Christian Ørsted and Michael Faraday
questions.explanation: Two major discoveries in electromagnetism: Hans Christian Ørsted (1820) discovered that an electric current flowing through a wire creates a magnetic field. He observed that a compass needle moved when placed near a current-carrying conductor. Michael Faraday (1831) discovered electromagnetic induction - that a changing magnetic field (like a moving magnet) can produce an electric current in a conductor. These discoveries form the foundation of modern electromagnetism and technologies like generators and transformers. Further Insights Faraday's law of electromagnetic induction is the basis of electric generators. Ørsted's discovery linked electricity and magnetism for the first time. James Clerk Maxwell later unified electricity and magnetism through Maxwell's equations. Electromagnetism is one of the four fundamental forces of nature. Modern power plants generate electricity using electromagnetic induction.

questions.question: What is the fixed maturity period for a Post Office Monthly Income Scheme (POMIS) account?
questions.explanation: The Post Office Monthly Income Scheme (POMIS) has a fixed maturity period of 5 years. Under this scheme: Investors deposit a lump sum. They receive monthly interest payouts. The principal amount is returned after 5 years. It is a popular low-risk savings option backed by the Government of India. Further Insights POMIS provides monthly interest payouts, unlike many schemes that compound annually. It is suitable for retirees seeking steady income. The scheme has a maximum investment limit (separate limits for single and joint accounts). Premature closure is allowed after 1 year with penalty conditions. Interest rates are revised quarterly by the Government.

questions.question: Mahesh Bhupathi made history in 1997 by becoming the first Indian to win a Grand Slam title. In which specific category did he achieve this feat at the French Open?
questions.explanation: In 1997, Mahesh Bhupathi won the French Open Mixed Doubles title partnering with Rika Hiraki of Japan. This made him: The first Indian to win a Grand Slam title A pioneer of India's success in international tennis This victory marked the beginning of India's strong presence in doubles tennis at the global level. Further Insights Leander Paes and Mahesh Bhupathi formed the famous "Indian Express" pair. The pair won multiple Grand Slam men's doubles titles between 1999 - 2001. Sania Mirza later became India's most successful female Grand Slam champion (mainly in doubles). The French Open is played on clay courts, which require strong baseline play. India has historically performed better in doubles rather than singles at Grand Slams.

questions.question: Which of the following acts has officially replaced the Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC), 1973?
questions.explanation: The Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS), 2023 officially replaced the Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC), 1973. CrPC dealt with procedural aspects of criminal law, such as: FIR registration Arrest procedures Investigation Bail Trial procedures BNSS now governs all these procedural aspects of criminal justice in India. Further Insights The three new criminal laws came into force on 1 July 2024. BNS replaces IPC (substantive criminal law). BNSS replaces CrPC (criminal procedure). BSA replaces the Indian Evidence Act (rules of evidence).

questions.question: In the context of the 1918 Influenza Pandemic (Spanish Flu) in India, which of the following best describes its impact on the Indian Nationalist Movement?
options: The pandemic reduced nationalist activities because Indian leaders mainly cooperated with the British government for relief work.
Public dissatisfaction over the British government's poor handling of the crisis and food shortages strengthened anti-colonial feelings.
Efficient British medical measures during the pandemic reduced political unrest and delayed the demand for complete independence.
The pandemic mainly weakened British officials, enabling revolutionaries to immediately take control of major cities.
questions.explanation: The 1918 Influenza pandemic caused millions of deaths in India. The British colonial government's response was widely seen as inadequate. At the same time: Food shortages continued. Grain exports were not effectively controlled. Relief measures were insufficient. This deepened public anger and contributed to growing distrust toward British rule. The crisis intensified anti-colonial sentiment, which later fed into movements like Non-Cooperation (1920). Further Insights The 1918 flu killed an estimated 12 - 18 million people in India. Mahatma Gandhi himself contracted influenza during this period. The crisis exposed weaknesses in colonial public health infrastructure. The post-war period (1919 onwards) saw the Rowlatt Act and Jallianwala Bagh, intensifying nationalism. Economic distress after World War I and the pandemic strengthened mass political mobilization.

questions.question: Who is the famous Indian author and Jnanpith Award winner, known for the novel Naukar Ki Kameez, who passed away in December 2025?
questions.explanation: Vinod Kumar Shukla was a renowned Hindi author and Jnanpith Award winner known for his novel Naukar Ki Kameez. He passed away on 23 December 2025 at the age of 88/89. He had a long literary career, and Naukar Ki Kameez is one of his best-known works. Further Insights Vinod Kumar Shukla was the first Jnanpith Awardee from Chhattisgarh. Naukar Ki Kameez was adapted into a film by director Mani Kaul. Shukla's writing style often combined everyday realism with poetic depth. He also received the Sahitya Akademi Award earlier in his career. His death was widely mourned across literary circles and public figures.

questions.question: With reference to the Gini Coefficient, consider the following statements: 1. It is a statistical measure primarily used to calculate the total wealth of a nation's population. 2. A Gini Coefficient value of "0" represents a state of perfect income equality within a society. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The Gini Coefficient does not measure total wealth. It measures income or wealth inequality within a population. It shows how evenly (or unevenly) income is distributed. A Gini value ranges from 0 to 1 (or sometimes expressed from 0 to 100). 0 = Perfect equality (everyone earns exactly the same income) 1 = Perfect inequality (one person has all the income) Further Insights The Gini Coefficient is derived from the Lorenz Curve. A higher Gini value indicates greater inequality. Nordic countries generally have low Gini values. Developing economies often show higher inequality levels. The Gini index does not capture absolute poverty - only distribution.

questions.question: The co-ordinates of the point P which divides the join of A (-6, 3) and B (7, 8) in the ratio 2 : 3 will be –
questions.explanation: " ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/stubeimg/7f68b277d8937af8fc7e14bc47bd9599.png) If the point P (x, y) divides the line segment A (a, b) and B (p, q) in the ratio m ∶ n, then P(x,y)=[(mp + na)/m + n, (mq + nb)/m + n] ---(1) As per the given question, A (a, b) = A (-6, 3) B (p, q) = B (7, 8) m = 2 and n = 3 Thus, from (1), we get P(x,y)=[(2 × 7) + (3 × −6)/2 + 3, (2 × 8) + (3 × 3)/2 + 3] ⇒P(x,y)=[(14 – 18)/5, (16 + 9)/5] ⇒P(x,y)=[−4/5,25/5] ⇒P(x,y)=[−0.8,5] "

questions.question: A can complete (25/2)% of a work in 2 days and B can complete (50/3)% of the same work in 3 days. They work together for 6 days and then B lefts the work. A alone will complete the remaining work in:
questions.explanation: Given: A can complete 25/2% of a work in 2 days and B can complete 50/3% work in 3 days. Calculation: 25/2% = 1/8 50/3% = 1/6 Let, the total work be 24 units. According to question, A can complete 3 units of work in 2 days. B can complete 4 units of work in 3 days. Efficiency of A = 3/2 units/day Efficiency of B = 4/3 units/day Efficiency of their work = 3/2 + 4/3 = 17/6 units/day According to question, They work for 6 days. Work completed in 6 days = [17/6 x 6] = 17 units Remaining work = 24 - 17 = 7 units So, A alone can complete the remaining work in 7/(3/2) days = 14/3 days

questions.question: If secA cos30° = tan60°, and A is an acute angle, then find the value of A.
questions.explanation: secA cos30° = tan60° secA * (√3/2) = √3 secA = 2 secA = sec60° A = 60°

questions.question: The difference between the second-year interest and the third-year interest on a sum of money deposited at 10% p.a. compound interest is Rs. 55. What was the total sum deposited?
questions.explanation: First year interest = 10% Second year interest = 10% + 10% X 10% = 11% Third year interest = 10% + 10% X (10% + 11%) = 12.10% 12.10% − 11% = 1.1% 1.1% of Sum = 55 Sum = Rs. 5000

questions.question: A and B start moving towards each other from places X and Y, respectively, at the same time. The speed of A is 20% more than that of B. After meeting on the way, A and B take 21_________2 hours and x hours, now to reach Y and X, respectively. What is the value of x?
questions.explanation: Let speed of B = m km/hr Then speed of A = m * 1.2 = 1.2m km/hr After meeting, ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p19217/image127.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p19217/image128.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p19217/image129.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p19217/image130.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p19217/image131.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p19217/image132.png)

questions.question: What is the number of arrangements if a dice rolled 44 times?
questions.explanation: When a dic e rolled N times, the number of arrangements = 6N Number of arrangements if a die rolled 44 times = 644

questions.question: Find the value of x if base area, volume and height of a prism are 9√3_________2 x3 cm 2, 21600cm 3 and 200√3 cm respectively.
questions.explanation: "Volume of prism = area of base × height ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/stubeimg/573e7463cee5dfb237b63b5c276626f4.png) "

questions.question: A cylinder is inscribed in a sphere whose total surface area is 841π cm2. Diameter of cylinder is 9 cm less than diameter of sphere. Find the volume of cylinder.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/other/ers23slt0408001.jpg) Let radius of circle = r cm Circumference = 841π cm 4πr2 = 841π r = 29/2 cm Diameter of sphere = 29cm Diameter of cylinder = 29 - 9 = 20 cm Let height of cylinder = H cm By Pythagoras theorem, H2 = (29)2 - (20)2 = 441 H = 21 cm Volume of cylinder = πr2H = 22/7* 10* 10* 21 = 6600 cm3

questions.question: After selling a chair, 10% profit is gained. If the chair was sold for ₹400 more, the profit percent would have been 30%. What would have been the selling price of the chair to earn 25% profit?
questions.explanation: profit% in first case = 10% profit% in second case = 30% Therefore, 20% profit = Rs.400 100% profit = 400 * 100/20 = rs.2000 So, the cost price of the chair = Rs.2000 Now, Profit% = 25% Selling price = 2000 x 125/100 = Rs.2500

questions.question: The ratio of the speed of a boat downstream to the speed of a stream is 4: 1. If a boat covered 352 km against the current in 22 hours, then find the speed of a boat in still water.
questions.explanation: Speed of a boat in still water = downstream speed of a boat - speed of a stream = (4x - x) km/hr = 3x km/hr Upstream speed of a boat = speed of a boat in still water - speed of a stream = (3x - x) km/hr = 2x km/hr Speed = Distance /Time Speed of a boat upstream = 352/22 2x = 16 x = 8 The speed of a boat in still water = 3 * 8 = 24 km/hr

questions.question: If Rs.1100 is obtained after lending out Rs.x at 5% per annum for 2 years at simple interest and Rs.1800 is obtained after lending out Rs. y at 10% per annum for 2 years at simple interest, find x + y.

The ratio of the number of books sold by shop A to that by shop C is 5: 7 and the sum of the number of books sold by shop B and shop C is 148. If the number of books sold by shop A is 4 less than that by shop B, then the number of books sold by shop B is:
questions.explanation: Let the number of books sold by shop A and shop C be 5a and 7a, respectively. The number of books sold by shop B = 148 - 7a 5a + 4 = 148 - 7a 12a = 144 a = 12 Hence, the required value = 5 * 12 + 4 = 64

questions.question: A circle is inscribed in a quadrilateral GHKL. If GH = 17.2 cm, HK = 15 cm, and KL = 18.4 cm, then what is the length of GL?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p11648/image007.jpg) We know that, When a circle touches all the four sides of a quadrilateral GHKL, then GH + KL = HK + GL => (17.2 + 18.4) = (15 + GL) => GL = 35.6 - 15 => GL = 20.6 cm

questions.question: A person bought an almirah for ₹29400 after 16% discount on its marked price. Find the marked price of the almirah.
questions.explanation: Let the marked price of the almirah = m Selling price of the almirah = ₹29400 m * (100-16)/100 = 29400 m * 84/100 = 29400 m = 29400 * 100/84 m = ₹35000

questions.question: If X is irrational and Y is a non-zero rational number, then 2X ÷ Y is:
questions.explanation: 2 is a rational number When Rational X Irrational = Irrational. So, 2X is irrational Irrational ÷ non-zero rational = Irrational

questions.question: If tan(A − B) = 1/√3 and sec(A + B) = 2, find 3A − 2B.

In an election only two candidates P and Q contested. 25% of the registered voters did not cast their votes and 250 votes cast were declared invalid. A gets 1150 votes more than B. Number of votes received by B is 1550. What is the number of registered voters?
questions.explanation: B received 1550 votes. A received = 1550+1150 = 2700 votes. Total valid votes = 1550+2700 = 4250 250 votes were invalid, hence total votes = 4500 Registered voters = 4500 x 100/75 = 6000

questions.question: Y and Z can do a work in 16 hours and 28 hours respectively. If they worked on alternate hours starting from Y, then in how many hours the whole work will complete?
questions.explanation: Total work = LCM of 16 and 28 = 112 units Efficiency of Y = 112/16 = 7 units/hour Efficiency of Z = 112/28 = 4 units/hour Work done in 20 hours = 10*(4 + 7) = 110 Remaining work = 112 - 110 = 2 units Time taken by Y and Z to complete the work = 20 + 2/7 = 20(2/7) hours

questions.question: A, B, and C start a business investing in the ratio 3: 4: 5. After 4 months, A withdraws his capital completely, while B and C continue for the full year. If total profit is ₹120,000, what is B's share?
questions.explanation: Profit share = capital X time A's capital * Time = 3k X 4 = Rs. 12k B's capital * Time = 4k X 12 = Rs. 48k C's capital * Time = 5k X 12 = Rs. 60k The ratio of profit share = 12k: 48k: 60k = 1: 4: 5 Hence, B's profit share = 4/10 X 120000 = ₹48,000

questions.question: Out of a solid cube of side 26cm the largest possible sphere is carved out. Find the volume of the sphere to the first place of decimal?
questions.explanation: For the largest possible sphere r = (1/2) * edge of solid cube = (1/2) * 26 = 13 cm the volume of sphere = (4/3) * π * r3 = (4/3) * (22/7) * 13 * 13 * 13 = 9206.4 cm3

questions.question: The marked price of an article is 5 1_________7 % more than its cost price. What maximum discount (nearest to whole number) can be offered by the shopkeeper to sell his article at no loss?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26358/image015.png) Let marked price be Rs.184 and cost price be Rs.175. Selling price of article at no loss = Cost price of article = Rs.175 Discount% = (184 - 175) * 100/184 = 900/184 = 4.89% = 5%

questions.question: The daily wages of a worker are Rs. 100. Five workers can do a work in 10 days. If you pay Rs. 20 more daily, they agree to do 25% more work daily. If the proposal is accepted, then the total amount that could be saved is
questions.explanation: Original total cost = 5 x 10 x 100 = Rs.5000 After the proposal, New daily wage = 100 + 20 = Rs.120 Doing 25% more work daily means: time required becomes 100/125 = 4/5 So, new time = 10 x 4/5 = 8 New total cost = 5 x 8 x 120 = Rs.4800 Amount saved = 5000 - 4800 = Rs.200

questions.question: The angle of elevation a bird from a point 240 m above a lake is 45° and the angle of depression of its reflection in the Lake is 60°, what is the height at which the bird is crated?
questions.explanation: " ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/stubeimg/8482713.png) Given that AP = 240 Let BE = x Then CD = x + 240 As we know = BD = AD AD = x - 240 tan60 = CD/AD √3 = (x+240)/(x-240) x+240 = x√3 - 240√3 x = 240( 2 + √3)

questions.question: What is the ratio in lowest terms of 6705 and 14155?
questions.explanation: Ratio in lowest terms of 6705 and 14155 = 6705 : 14155 = 9 : 19

questions.question: In two bowls, there is a mixture of sea salt and sugar powders. The ratio of sea salt and sugar powders in these bowls is 10: 11 and 5: 9, respectively. In what ratio should these mixture be mixed so that the ratio of sea salt and sugar powders in the new bowl becomes 3: 4 respectively?
questions.explanation: 1st bowl has 10/21 part of sea salt powder and 2nd bowl has 5/14 part of sea salt powder. By Allegation method, ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p20296/image003.jpg) The ratio of powder mixed = (10/21 - 3/7)/(3/7 - 5/14) = 1/14: 1/21 = 21: 14 = 3: 2

questions.question: Select the synonym of 'Luscious':
questions.explanation: 'Luscious' means '(of food or drink) having a pleasingly rich, sweet taste.'. Options b) and c) can be rejected as they are unrelated to the word. 'Unappetizing' means 'Not inviting or attractive; unwholesome.'. Hence, option d) can also be rejected as it is the exact opposite. 'Solitude' means 'The state or situation of being alone.'. 'Unscrupulous' means 'Having or showing no moral principles; not honest or fair.'. 'Delicious' means 'highly pleasant to the taste.'. Hence, option a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select No improvement required. Did you practiced the piano lessons this morning?
questions.explanation: The verbs 'do, does, did' are followed by base verb. The correct sentence is- Did you practice the piano lessons this morning? Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.question: Which of the following sentences is grammatically correct?
options: If it rains, we will cancel the picnic.
If it will rain, we cancel the picnic.
If it rains, we would cancel the picnic.
If it would rain, we will cancel the picnic.
questions.explanation: The correct sentence is (a). This is first conditional for real future possibility: present simple in if-clause, "will" + base verb in main clause. Option (b) is incorrect because if-clause uses present simple ("rains"), not future "will rain"; main clause should have "will." Option (c) is incorrect as "would" implies second conditional (unreal), but here it's a real possibility. Option (d) is incorrect mixing second ("would") and first ("will") conditionals inconsistently. Hence, the correct answer is option (a).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined word in the following sentence. The sagacious old professor shared her wisdom with the eager students, guiding them with insightful advice.
questions.explanation: Sagacious: having or showing acute mental discernment and keen practical sense. Ignorant: lacking in knowledge or training; unlearned. Wise: having the power of discerning and judging properly as to what is true or right. Sorrowful: full of or feeling sorrow; grieved; sad. Careless: not paying enough attention to what one does. Among the given options, the word 'wise' can correctly substitute the underlined word. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: The figure of speech used in "The classroom was a fish market..."?
questions.explanation: Option 1: A metaphor directly compares two different things by saying one is the other. In this case, the classroom is being compared to a zoo, implying noise, chaos, and disorder. No comparison words (like, as) are used, which makes it a metaphor. Option 2: A simile compares two things using like or as. Example: "The classroom was like a zoo." Since the given line does not use like or as, it cannot be a simile. Option 3: Hyperbole is an exaggeration used for emphasis. Example: "I'm so hungry I could eat a horse." The line "The classroom was a zoo" is not an exaggeration but a direct comparison, so it is not hyperbole. Option 4: Alliteration is the repetition of initial consonant sounds in closely placed words. Example: "She sells seashells by the seashore." The sentence does not have repeated consonant sounds, so it is not alliteration. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.question: Find the part of the sentence that contains an error. He said to me that he was focusing (1)/ on batting even though (2)/ his bowling figures have not been (3)/ impressive in the ongoing tournament. (4)
options: his bowling figures have not been (3)
He said to me that he was focusing (1)
questions.explanation: Part A has an error. When used with a that-clause, 'tell' takes an indirect object, while 'say' does not. Example sentence: Ramya told me (that) she would arrive early today. (not: told (that)) Ramya said (that) she would arrive early today. (not: said to me) Therefore, in the given sentence, the phrase 'said to me' is incorrect. The correct sentence is: He told me that he was focusing on batting even though his bowling figures have not been impressive in the ongoing tournament. Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to improve the bold part of the sentence. Suppose if you get selected in the competitive exam, will you throw a grand party?
questions.explanation: The use of 'suppose' and 'if' in a single sentence is redundant as they both are talking about an assumption. So, 'if' will be used. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.question: Find the part of the sentence that contains an error. Tanmay asked her that (1)/ whether she knew (2)/ what had happened last month (3)/ when he was on leave. (4)
questions.explanation: The error lies in part A of the sentence. In any sentence 'that' and 'whether' cannot be used together. Therefore, remove 'that' since 'asked' is mentioned in the sentencewhich justifies the usage of 'whether'. The correct sentence is: " Tanmay asked her whether she knew what had happened last month when he was on leave." Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.question: Choose the appropriate antonym of "Impeccable".
questions.explanation: The word 'impeccable' is an adjective meaning without mistakes or faults. The most apt antonym of the given word is 'imperfect' (containing faults or mistakes; not complete or perfect). Hence, option (c) is the correct answer. Superficial - seeing only what is obvious Ideal - perfect; most suitable Unblemished - not spoiled, damaged or marked in any way

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Politeness is to do and say the kindest thing _____ the kindest way.
questions.explanation: Such a preposition is to be used that can relate well the kindest thing with the kindest way. So, 'in' is the correct choice because here 'in' is used to express 'do and say' the kindest thing in the kindest way as an integral part of an activity, i.e., politeness. 'On' is used to express physically in contact with and supported by (a surface). 'To' is used to express motion in the direction of (a particular location). 'Of' is used to express the relationship between a part and a whole. Hence, option (d) is correct. Note: A Preposition is a word placed before a noun or a pronoun or a noun equivalent (pronoun, adverb, gerund, infinitive, adverbial clause, or any clause that can be used as the object of the preposition) and shows a relationship in space or time or a logical relationship between two or more people, places, or things in a sentence. Examples 1) The teacher scolded the child for being late. 2) We're going to a party. · The words in bold are called prepositions because they normally take position before (pre) a noun/pronoun or a noun equivalent (pronoun, adverb, gerund, infinitive, adverbial clause, or any clause that can be used as the object of the preposition. · The noun/pronoun which is used with a preposition is called its Object.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with an appropriate degree of comparison. The team that makes the ____________ mistakes will win the game.
questions.explanation: The sentence structure implies that one team is being singled out from a group (all the teams playing). It's the team that makes the minimum number of mistakes. This requires the superlative degree. The definite article "the" before the blank is a strong clue that a superlative is needed. The blank modifies the noun "mistakes." "Mistakes" is a countable noun (you can have one mistake, two mistakes, etc.). The correct adjective for quantity with countable nouns is few. The degrees of comparison for "few" are: Positive: few Comparative: fewer Superlative: fewest The complete sentence is: The team that makes the fewest mistakes will win the game. Hence, option (c) is the answer. Option a) few: This is the positive degree and does not show the necessary level of comparison. Option b) fewer: This is the comparative degree, used to compare only two things (e.g., "This team made fewer mistakes than the other team"). The sentence implies a comparison among a larger group. Option d) less: This is a very common grammar error. "Less" is the comparative form of "little" and is used for uncountable nouns (like time, sugar, or water). Since "mistakes" is a countable noun, the correct term is "fewer" (for comparative) or "fewest" (for superlative). Note: · The Positive Degree is used to denote the mere existence of some quality of what we speak about. It is used when no comparison is made. · The Comparative degree denotes a higher degree of quality than the positive and is used when two things are compared. · The Superlative degree indicates the highest degree of quality and is used when more than two things (or sets of things) are compared. Countable and uncountable nouns: 1. Countable nouns are the class of nouns that can be counted, for example, 'table' - one table, two tables, three tables, etc. 2. Uncountable nouns are the nouns that cannot be counted or usually are not counted, for example, light, water, milk, information, furniture, etc. They do not generally have a plural form and can't be preceded by 'a' or 'an'. Many abstract nouns are typically uncountable. Some uncountable nouns can be used in the plural as well, depending on the meaning or context of the word.

questions.question: Which feature most clearly indicates a genuine hiring post on social media, as per the passage?
options: High number of likes and comments
questions.explanation: Solution: The passage states, "One of the clearest indicators of a genuine hiring post is the presence of specific and verifiable details," such as job title, qualifications, and application process. This directly supports option (c). Hence, (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: How can job seekers verify the credibility of a hiring announcement, as per the passage?
questions.explanation: Solution: The passage notes that credibility can be confirmed by checking the organisation's official website or careers section, which helps verify whether the position actually exists. Hence, (c) is the correct answer. Reference line: "Checking the organisation's official website or careers section can quickly confirm whether the advertised position actually exists."

questions.question: Which of the following best captures the central idea of the passage in a distinctive way?
options: Social media has simplified recruitment processes
questions.explanation: Solution: The passage consistently emphasises the need for job seekers to verify sources, scrutinise details, and prioritise official application channels in order to distinguish genuine opportunities from misleading posts. This idea is best captured by option (b). Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: The tone of the passage can best be described as:
questions.explanation: Solution: The passage repeatedly warns job seekers to exercise "critical awareness" and "informed caution" while navigating social media job posts. This advisory guidance gives the passage a cautionary tone. Hence, (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the passage, the word "scrutinising" most nearly means:
questions.explanation: Solution: The passage advises job seekers to verify sources and "scrutinise details", implying careful and detailed examination to distinguish genuine posts from misleading ones. Hence, "scrutinising" here means examining closely, making option (b) the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: The incorrectly spelt word is 'Absense'. The correct spelling is "Absence," meaning the state of being away or not present. Other words are correctly spelt. 'Parallel' refers to lines or surfaces that are always the same distance apart. 'Criticism' refers to the act of judging the merits and faults of something. 'Category' is a class or division of people or things. Hence, (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the correct Synonym of "WILT".
questions.explanation: The word "Wilt" means to become limp or weak. 1) "Droop" - correct synonym; to sag or lose strength. 2) "Flourish" - opposite; to thrive. 3) "Thrive" - opposite; to grow vigorously. 4) "Bloom" - opposite; to prosper or blossom. Hence, option (a) is the answer.

questions.question: Parts of a sentence are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the parts in the correct order to form a meaningful sentence. (a) will be matched by comprehensive (b) your drive, energy, and desire to excel (c) formal sales skills training and (d) strong commercial acumen
questions.explanation: The sentence talks about energy required to excel in sales. '(b)' talks about the main subject that is 'your drive', and the action that has to be taken regarding the same is mentioned in (a). 'c' and 'd' is a mandatory pair as 'c' ends with 'and' which shows that more additional skills are required which is further described in 'd'. The correct sequence of the parts of sentence is b,a,c,d. The sentence is - "Your drive, energy, and desire to excel will be matched by comprehensive formal sales skills training and strong commercial acumen." Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.question: Rearrange the parts of this sentence: enormous practical and (P) the sudden shift (Q) to e-books had (R) financial implications (S)
questions.explanation: Two mandatory pairs QR and PS would be formed. QR would be formed because R describes the sudden shift that took place in the field of books and PS would be formed because both the adjectives 'enormous' and 'practical' mentioned in P modifies the noun 'implications' mentioned in S. Since 'had' acts as the verb, it would take the object after it which is in PS so QR would precede PS. The correct sequence is: QRPS. Rearranged sentence: The sudden shift to e-books had enormous practical and financial implications. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.question: Which of the following refers to the science of improving a human population by controlled breeding?
questions.explanation: "Eugenics" is the science of improving the genetic quality of a human population through controlled breeding to promote desired traits and reduce the prevalence of undesirable traits. "Genetics" is the scientific study of genes, heredity, and variation in living organisms. "Anthropology" is the study of humans, their societies, cultures, and biological development over time. "Evolution" is the process by which species change over time through natural selection and genetic variation. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.question: Select the correctly spelt word to fill in the blank. Only employees and their family members can enjoy the _______.
questions.explanation: Among the given options, the word with the correct spelling that can fill the given blank is option (c), 'concession'. The correct sentence is: Only employees and their family members can enjoy the concession. Concession- a thing that is granted, especially in response to demands Hence, the correct answer is option (c).

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct speech. He told me that he had accepted a new job offer with an exciting company in the city.
options: He said to me, "I have accepted a new job offer with an exciting company in the city."
He said to me, "I will accept a new job offer with an exciting company in the city."
He said to me, "I will have accepted a new job offer with an exciting company in the city."
He says to me, "I accepted a new job offer with an exciting company in the city."
questions.explanation: The sentence in the Indirect Speech is in Past Perfect Tense. There are three cases when the Indirect Speech is in Past Perfect Tense. · Tense remains same · Past perfect changes to Present Perfect · Past perfect changes to Simple Past Case 1: Past Perfect (Subject + had + V3 + Object) tense will be kept intact. For example: He said that he had just finished the movie. (Indirect Speech) He said, "I had just finished the movie" (Direct Speech) Case 2: Past Perfect (Subject + had + V3 +Object) will be converted into Present Perfect (Subject + has/have + V3 +Object). For example: He said that he had got a new job. (Indirect Speech) He said, "I have got a new job." (Direct Speech) Case 3: Past Perfect (Subject + had + V3 + Object) will be converted into Past Simple (Subject + V2 + Object). For example: He said that Arya had sent him a book. (Indirect Speech) He said, "Arya sent me a book" (Direct Speech) As per the options, we have to go with Case 2. The following things in the sentence will change when changed from Indirect Speech to Direct Speech: "Told me" will be changed to "said to me". "Says to me" will be incorrect, hence, option d) is eliminated. "He had" will be changed to "I have". Therefore, the correct answer is option a): He said to me, "I have accepted a new job offer with an exciting company in the city."

questions.question: Choose the correct indirect speech form of the following sentence. I said to him, "Let us get you up to the emergency care ward."
options: I asked him that we should get him to the emergency care ward.
I ordered us to get him to the emergency care ward.
I suggested that they should get me to the emergency care ward.
I suggested to him that we should get him up to the emergency care ward.
questions.explanation: Option (d) is the correct answer. "Let" is used to mean "to allow" (request) or "to propose/suggest". "Let" as a proposal/suggestion: Rules to follow: • "Said/said to" is changed to "proposed/proposed to" or "suggested/suggested to" • 'That' is added in indirect speech. • "Let" is removed in the indirect speech. • The modal verb "should" is added in indirect speech. • Pronouns 'us' and 'you' are changed into 'we' and 'him' respectively. Example 1: He said, "Let us go to the amusement park." (Direct Speech) He suggested that they should go to the amusement park. (Indirect Speech) Example 2: I said to him, "Let us attend the office party." (Direct Speech) I proposed to him that we should attend the office party. (Indirect Speech) Therefore, the correct answer is: I suggested to him that we should get him up to the emergency care ward.

questions.question: What does the idiom 'day in the sun' means?
options: getting all the attention you want
questions.explanation: The idiom 'day in the sun' means to get a lot of attention. E.g. Enjoy your day in the sun while you can; a better singer will soon come along.
